subject: current-affairs
description: Kashmir Siachen & Sir Creek MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: Kashmir was purchased by Gulab Singh from the British Government for:
mcqs.explanation: Under the Treaty of Amritsar (1846), Gulab Singh purchased Kashmir from the British for Rs. 7.5 million (Nanakshahi rupees).

mcqs.q: The first battle for freedom against the arbitrary and repressive rule of the Dogra dynasty was fought in:
mcqs.explanation: The first organized battle for freedom against Dogra rule in Kashmir was fought in 1930.

mcqs.q: The 'Chenab Formula' to resolve the Kashmir dispute was presented by:
mcqs.explanation: The Chenab Formula — a proposed partition of Kashmir along the Chenab river — was presented by Sardar Abdul Qayyum Khan of Azad Kashmir.

mcqs.q: The Line of Control in Kashmir was drawn in:
mcqs.explanation: The LoC was drawn in 1972 under the Simla Agreement signed between India and Pakistan after the 1971 war.

mcqs.q: The total length of the 'Line of Control' in Kashmir is about:
mcqs.explanation: The Line of Control runs for about 720 km through Jammu & Kashmir.

mcqs.q: Where is the Siachen Glacier located?
mcqs.explanation: The Siachen Glacier is located in the Karakoram mountain range.

mcqs.q: Which two countries claim the Siachen Glacier?
mcqs.explanation: The Siachen Glacier is claimed by both Pakistan and India, with active troop deployments on both sides since 1984.

mcqs.q: What does the word 'Siachen' mean?
mcqs.explanation: 'Siachen' in the local Balti language means 'place of wild roses'.

mcqs.q: When was the Cease-Fire Line drawn in Kashmir?
mcqs.explanation: The Cease-Fire Line in Kashmir was drawn in 1948 following the first Indo-Pak war over Kashmir.

mcqs.q: All Parties Hurriyat Conference (APHC) is an alliance of:
mcqs.explanation: APHC is an alliance of 23 political, social and religious parties from Indian-administered Jammu & Kashmir.

mcqs.q: Out of 14 districts of Jammu & Kashmir, the Muslim-majority districts are:
mcqs.explanation: Out of 14 districts of J&K, 10 are Muslim-majority districts.

mcqs.q: The percentage of Muslims in Jammu & Kashmir is approximately:
mcqs.explanation: Muslims make up approximately 80% of the population of Jammu & Kashmir.

mcqs.q: The disputed territory between India and Pakistan after the partition of the Indian Subcontinent in August 1947 was:
mcqs.explanation: Kashmir has been the primary disputed territory between India and Pakistan since the partition of 1947.

mcqs.q: When did Maharaja Hari Singh sign a standstill agreement with Pakistan?
mcqs.explanation: Maharaja Hari Singh signed a standstill agreement with Pakistan in June 1947 prior to partition.

mcqs.q: Which declaration was issued by India and Pakistan after the 1965 war?
mcqs.explanation: The Tashkent Declaration, signed in January 1966, formally ended the 1965 Indo-Pak war.

mcqs.q: India revised the Kashmir dispute in the UNO in 1948. When did the UN pass resolutions granting Kashmiris the right of plebiscite?
mcqs.explanation: The UN Security Council passed two plebiscite resolutions on Kashmir — on 13 August 1948 and on 5 January 1949.

mcqs.q: The APHC (All Parties Hurriyat Conference) was formed in Jammu & Kashmir in:
mcqs.explanation: APHC was formed in April 1993 as an alliance of 23 pro-freedom parties in Jammu & Kashmir.

mcqs.q: The boundary line which includes the creek in Sindh (Sir Creek area) is known as the:
mcqs.explanation: The Green Line marks the disputed boundary between Sindh (Pakistan) and Gujarat (India) including Sir Creek.

mcqs.q: A massive avalanche claimed the lives of more than a hundred Pakistani soldiers in Siachen on:
mcqs.explanation: The Gayari avalanche in Siachen occurred on 7 April 2012.

mcqs.q: The Siachen Glacier in mountainous Kashmir is known as the world's highest:
mcqs.explanation: Siachen is known as the world's highest battlefield — where India and Pakistan have maintained troop deployments above 5,000 metres since 1984.

mcqs.q: Siachen has been the site of fierce fighting between nuclear-armed rivals Pakistan and India since:
mcqs.explanation: Active military conflict on Siachen began in 1984 when India launched Operation Meghdoot to occupy the glacier.

mcqs.q: Where is the Siachen Glacier situated?
mcqs.explanation: The Siachen Glacier is situated in the Baltistan region of Gilgit-Baltistan.

mcqs.q: The Siachen camp of Pakistani troops is located in:
mcqs.explanation: The Pakistan Army's Siachen base camp is located in the Gayari sector — where the 2012 avalanche struck.

mcqs.q: India and Pakistan fought a fierce battle over Siachen in ______, raising fears of all-out conflict.
mcqs.explanation: In 1987, India and Pakistan fought a major battle over Siachen (the Bilafond La/Op Rajiv–Qaidat-e-Shaheed clashes), raising fears of wider war.

mcqs.q: Guns on the Siachen Glacier have largely fallen silent since a peace process was launched between Pakistan and India in:
mcqs.explanation: The Siachen guns have largely fallen silent since the composite peace dialogue launched in 2004.

mcqs.q: How many total persons were buried alive as the avalanche hit the Pakistan Army camp in Siachen in 2012?
mcqs.explanation: The 2012 Gayari avalanche buried 135 people alive in total — 124 soldiers and 11 civilian workers.

mcqs.q: How many civilians were buried alive when the avalanche hit the Pakistan Army camp in Siachen?
mcqs.explanation: Eleven civilian workers were among those buried in the 2012 Gayari avalanche.

mcqs.q: How many army soldiers were buried alive when the avalanche hit the Pakistan Army camp in Siachen?
mcqs.explanation: 124 Pakistan Army soldiers were buried alive in the 2012 Gayari Siachen avalanche.

mcqs.q: Siachen is close to four of the world's 14 peaks above 8,000 metres — K2, Broad Peak, Gasherbrum I and ______ — all on the Pakistani side of the frontline.
mcqs.explanation: The fourth 8,000-metre peak near Siachen (on the Pakistani side) is Gasherbrum II.

mcqs.q: 12 of the world's 30 highest mountains are located in which range?
mcqs.explanation: 12 of the world's 30 highest peaks — including K2, the world's second-highest mountain — are in the Karakoram range.

mcqs.q: Name the longest glacier of Pakistan.
mcqs.explanation: Siachen is the longest glacier in Pakistan at about 72.5 km.

mcqs.q: What is the total length of the Siachen Glacier?
mcqs.explanation: The Siachen Glacier is approximately 72.5 km long.

mcqs.q: The Siachen Glacier is situated in the mountain range of:
mcqs.explanation: Siachen lies in the Karakoram mountain range.

mcqs.q: Name the highest glacier of Pakistan.
mcqs.explanation: Siachen is also the highest glacier in Pakistan by altitude.

mcqs.q: The Siachen Glacier is the longest glacier in the Karakoram and the ____ longest in the world's non-polar areas.
mcqs.explanation: Siachen is the 2nd longest glacier in the world's non-polar regions (after Fedchenko Glacier in Tajikistan).

mcqs.q: When did the Rann of Kutch arbitration take place?
mcqs.explanation: The Rann of Kutch arbitration tribunal delivered its verdict in 1968.

mcqs.q: In what year did the Rann of Kutch arbitration award Pakistan 10% of its claim over 9,000 sq km?
mcqs.explanation: The 1968 arbitration verdict awarded Pakistan roughly 10% of the 9,000 sq km it had claimed.

mcqs.q: The Sir Creek dispute hinges on the interpretation of the line between the Rann of Kutch and Sindh as depicted in which maps?
mcqs.explanation: The dispute over Sir Creek is based on interpretation of the 1914 and 1925 maps that show the boundary between the Rann of Kutch and Sindh.

mcqs.q: The name of the highest battlefield in Pakistan as well as in the world is:
mcqs.explanation: Siachen is universally referred to as the highest battlefield in Pakistan and the world.

mcqs.q: What is the total length of Sir Creek?
mcqs.explanation: Sir Creek is a 60-mile tidal estuary in the Rann of Kutch.

mcqs.q: Which Article of the Indian Constitution was scrapped by the Indian Parliament on 5 August 2019, stripping IIOJK of its special status?
mcqs.explanation: Article 370 was revoked by the Indian Parliament on 5 August 2019, ending the special autonomous status of Jammu & Kashmir.

mcqs.q: Which of the following Articles was revoked alongside Article 370?
mcqs.explanation: Article 35A — which permitted J&K to define its permanent residents — was revoked alongside Article 370 on 5 August 2019.

mcqs.q: After the bifurcation of Jammu & Kashmir in 2019, which of the following became a new Union Territory without a legislature?
mcqs.explanation: Ladakh was declared a new Union Territory without a legislature, while Jammu & Kashmir became a Union Territory with a legislature.

mcqs.q: Indian aircraft violated the Line of Control and were scrambled back after timely PAF response on:
mcqs.explanation: Indian aircraft violated the LoC on 26 February 2019 and were forced to return after the PAF scrambled its jets.

mcqs.q: The Pakistan Air Force (PAF) shot down how many Indian jets on 27 February 2019?
mcqs.explanation: PAF shot down 2 Indian Air Force jets on 27 February 2019 inside Pakistani airspace.

mcqs.q: Wing Commander Noman Ali Khan shot down one Indian jet on 27 February 2019. The PAF Squadron Leader who shot down the second Indian jet was:
mcqs.explanation: Squadron Leader Hassan Siddiqui shot down the second Indian Air Force jet on 27 February 2019.

mcqs.q: The district of AJ&K (Azad Jammu & Kashmir) where an Indian aircraft crashed on 27 February 2019 was:
mcqs.explanation: The Indian aircraft crashed in Kohi Ratta, Kotli District, AJ&K on 27 February 2019.

mcqs.q: Two Indian Air Force (IAF) aircraft were shot down by the PAF inside Pakistani airspace on 27 February 2019. One was a Sukhoi Su-30MKI; the second was a:
mcqs.explanation: The second IAF aircraft shot down on 27 February 2019 was a MiG-21 Bison.

mcqs.q: Which Indian pilot was captured by the Pakistan Army on 27 February 2019?
mcqs.explanation: Wing Commander Abhinandan Varthaman of the Indian Air Force was captured by the Pakistan Army on 27 February 2019 after his MiG-21 was shot down.

mcqs.q: On which date did the Prime Minister of Pakistan announce the release of Indian pilot Abhinandan as a gesture of peace?
mcqs.explanation: Prime Minister Imran Khan announced the release of Indian pilot Abhinandan on 28 February 2019 as a 'gesture of peace'.

mcqs.q: On which date was Indian pilot Abhinandan released?
mcqs.explanation: Indian pilot Abhinandan was released and handed over at the Wagah border on 1 March 2019.

mcqs.q: Squadron Leader Hassan Siddiqui was featured in a Defence Day 2016 video song paying tribute to the bravery and professionalism of the Pakistan Air Force. The song's title was:
mcqs.explanation: Squadron Leader Hassan Siddiqui was featured in the PAF Defence Day 2016 tribute video song 'Fizaon Ke Mohafiz'.

mcqs.q: Who was the Director General of ISPR (Inter-Services Public Relations) when the 27 Feb 2019 events took place?
mcqs.explanation: Maj Gen Asif Ghafoor was the DG ISPR during the 27 February 2019 India-Pakistan aerial engagement and the public announcement of the captured pilot Abhinandan.

mcqs.q: Omar Abdullah was sworn in as Chief Minister of Indian Illegally Occupied Jammu & Kashmir (IIOJK) on ____, 2024.
mcqs.explanation: Omar Abdullah took oath as Chief Minister of IIOJK on 16 October 2024 — the first elected CM after Article 370 was scrapped.

mcqs.q: Omar Abdullah, the Chief Minister of IIOJK, belongs to which political party?
mcqs.explanation: Omar Abdullah is a senior leader of the Jammu & Kashmir National Conference (JKNC), the party founded by his grandfather Sheikh Abdullah.

mcqs.q: Which international body released two reports on 'the situation of human rights in Indian-administered Kashmir and Pakistan-administered Kashmir'?
mcqs.explanation: The UN Office of the High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR) released two major reports on the human rights situation in Indian-administered Kashmir and Pakistan-administered Kashmir.
